nodes.label: P4.1 — ambient-dim tolerance (A2) — OURS's FIRST WIN
nodes.description: Fixed difficulty (Bayes flat 0.108), ambient dim swept 8->500 (signal in a 2-D subspace, rest nuisance). OURS holds ~0.79 while BP gently declines and Mono-Forward collapses toward chance (0.75->0.41, overfits). OURS crosses ABOVE tuned BP by dim 500 (gap -0.029, all-negative IQR) -- cheaper and more accurate. Mechanism: contrast+layernorm down-weights non-discriminative dims for free.

nodes.label: P4.2 — depth headroom (A3) x difficulty — depth-composition GENERALIZES
nodes.description: Headroom task (make_tierb, 64 clusters), difficulty overlap 0.4-1.2, L=8; per-layer probe slope, OURS w=2 vs w=1 (control) vs GD-hidden. OURS w=2 slope POSITIVE across the whole difficulty band (IQR disjoint from 0); w=1 never composes (coordination is the lever, robustly); GD loses its own headroom by overlap 1.0. OURS out-composes BP-hidden in the hard/low-headroom regime (crossover ~overlap 0.6).

nodes.label: P4.3 — width x depth (A4) — WIN (cost); re-run+extended with decay finding
nodes.description: Iso-weight-budget shape sweep L2->L12, three racers OLD(energy-Sigma h^2)/OURS(contrast+w2)/BP, headline=last-layer readout, plus fixed-width W64 control. Backward cost flat in depth for OLD/OURS (14-40k) vs BP linear (52k->169k, up to 6.8x costlier). OLD collapses monotonically with depth (the wall, made legible). OURS composes to ~L3-L5, beats BP L2-L6 on headroom, then DECAYS past L8 -- confirmed to be depth-decay not width-shrink via the W64 control.
nodes.rationale: Establishes that the deployed all-tap/boosting readout is load-bearing (a single deep head would be the wrong design) and that energy-Sigma h^2 is decisively closed as a depth substrate.

nodes.label: P4.3 follow-up — why it decays: local-objective drift, not capacity/width
nodes.description: Widening each layer with depth (up to W240) does NOT fix the decay (fix64 and widen converge by L12: 0.449 vs 0.441). Dead-unit fraction ~0 at every layer; widen carries higher effective rank (18.7 vs 12.3 @L12) at IDENTICAL accuracy -> decay is local-objective drift off the class manifold past ~layer 5, not dead units or capacity. A mixed flat+headroom task shows deep layers CORRUPT an early-solved flat subtask while tuned BP holds it flat.
nodes.rationale: Verdict: useful composition is ~5 layers; the fix is preservation (all-tap/boosting, vindicated, or residual skips), not width -- this open wound becomes Phase 5.

nodes.label: P4.4 — class count (A5) + real anchors — competitive-but-trails, difficulty-gated
nodes.description: Synthetic C in {2,4,10,20}: gap widens +0.06->+0.23 (saturating ~C10), OURS's edge over Mono erodes (ties by C20). But real anchors are far kinder: digits (10-class) gap only +0.027, CIFAR-flat +0.090 -- the synthetic widening is discrimination hardness, not a many-class penalty.

nodes.label: P4.5 — continual (A6) across difficulty — THE decisive WIN
nodes.description: Class-incremental digits stream at swept synthetic difficulty + digits anchor, 3 seeds. OURS+sleep forgets almost nothing (BWT -0.02 to -0.18) at EVERY difficulty while online-BP catastrophically forgets (-0.83 to -0.99); no-sleep rots (sleep is the mechanism). Digits reproduces P3.3 exactly (0.954/-0.017). BWT stays excellent even as AA falls with difficulty -- the win doesn't erode off the home config.
nodes.rationale: The architecture's reason for being; the caveat is the baseline is naive online-BP without replay -- a fairer BP+replay comparison is deferred to Stage 2 (P10).

nodes.label: P4.6 — noise robustness (A7) — an HONEST NEGATIVE
nodes.description: Train clean, inject multiplicative Gaussian weight noise at eval, sweep sigma 0-0.4. Hypothesis REFUTED: OURS is the LEAST robust (retention 0.754 vs BP 0.875 at sigma=0.4), not more robust than tuned BP. Likely cause: per-sample layernorm (A2's robustness mechanism) doesn't damp weight-DIRECTION noise -- A2's win and A7's loss share a cause (a real tradeoff).
nodes.rationale: Eval-time noise on a clean-trained model is NOT the substrate's regime (online learning WITH noise / hardware-aware is untested) -- flagged as the architecture's sharpest open silicon risk, addressed in Phase 6.

nodes.label: P4.7 — synthesis: the capability map + hand-off brief; PHASE 4 CLOSED
nodes.description: Assembles the 7 axis tiles into the capability map. Hand-off brief: (1) optimize the continual mechanism (sleep cadence + Ch7 gate) -> Stage 2 core; (2) build deep but gate depth on headroom, scale coordination window with headroom -> Phase 5 closes the decay; (3) make the cost meter depth-aware and temporal; (4) run train-with-noise before any noise-robustness claim -> Phase 6; (5) validate multi-class on natural data -> Phase 10; (6) don't compete on static accuracy/many-class/eval-time noise.
